text: Welcome to another video. Today, we're reviewing primary abandonment of the hernia sac in patients with inguinal scleral hernias. This is a technique that's been described over the course of the last few years, where you incise the peritoneum around the deep ring and address that fenestration at the completion of the case. You dissociate the distal sac and minimize bleeding and tissue trauma related to sac dissection around the cord elements. you can reduce operating room time variability, and it has been associated with some good outcomes from a publication by Morel et al.

The surgeon makes an incision on the cephalad portion of the deep ring in a patient with a large inguinal hernia, serving as a marker for sac abandonment. The flap development is initiated to facilitate mesh placement, followed by lateral dissection around the deep ring. The intentional hole is visible, and medial dissection is performed to mobilize the peritoneum and complete the sac abandonment.

The surgeon focuses on grabbing the peritoneum and dissecting away the cord structures and neurovascular elements. A circumferential incision is made around the deep ring, allowing for a clear view of the myopectineal orifice. The dissection continues around the vas, with the vasculature being parietalized. The external iliac vein is identified, and a small cord lipoma is transected to reveal the iliopubic tract. The dissection is finalized, preparing the area for mesh placement and positioning.

The surgeon places a heavyweight, extra-large mesh into the prepared pocket for inguinal sclerodal hernias. The mesh is fixated at Cooper's ligament and the superior edge. The flap is closed from lateral to medial, ensuring not to integrate too much of the flap into the primary closure to maintain redundancy for the fenestrations.

I do believe that it has reduced variation in operating room time and has been associated with good outcomes None of my patients have had significant seromas or required any procedural interventions. And I've been doing this now for about a year. I'm really happy with the initial outcomes. Thank you.
